solarpunk.ponder.andesite_panel_usage.te: The Andesite Solar Panel generates Rotational Force from sunlight
It outputs rotation downwards — connect a shaft or machine directly below
At dawn and dusk: 8 RPM with 1024 SU of stress capacity
At noon in clear weather: 16 RPM with 4096 SU of stress capacity
Rain reduces output to dawn levels — night stops generation entirely
The panel must have a clear view of the sky directly above to function
solarpunk.ponder.biofuel_engine_usage.te: The Biofuel Engine burns Biofuel to generate Rotational Force
Pipe Biofuel into any face to fill the internal tank
With Biofuel present, it outputs 16 RPM with 8192 SU of stress capacity
Rotation exits downward — connect a shaft or machine directly below
The Biomass Gasifier burns Biomass to generate Rotational Force
Add Biomass into the top slot — each piece burns for 15 seconds
While burning, it outputs 8 RPM with 2048 SU of stress capacity
Rotation exits downward — connect a shaft or machine directly below
Output is intermittent — use a Kinetic Battery to buffer power between refills
solarpunk.ponder.brass_panel_usage.text_: The Brass Solar Panel generates Forge Energy (FE) directly from sunlight
Connect cables or conduits to any face to transfer the generated power
At dawn and dusk: 40 FE/t — at noon in clear weather: 80 FE/t
Rain reduces output to dawn levels — the panel must have clear sky access above it
solarpunk.ponder.fermentation_vat_scalin: A larger footprint increases the batch size — a 2x2 vat processes 4 Biomass at once, a 3x3 processes 9
Water consumed and Biofuel produced per batch scale with the footprint area
Tank capacity grows with each block added — taller vats hold more fluid
Stack Fermentation Vat blocks — they merge into a single multiblock
The vat needs at least a 2x2 footprint to ferment anything
Pipe water in through any face to fill the water tank
Insert Biomass into the input from the top
The vat ferments Biomass with water into Biofuel — a 2x2 vat consumes 4 Biomass per batch
Drain Biofuel from any face and pipe it to a Biofuel Engine
solarpunk.ponder.geyser_cap_usage.text_1: Geyser Vents naturally generate in hot, arid biomes
solarpunk.ponder.geyser_cap_usage.text_2: Place a Geyser Cap directly on top of a Geyser Vent to harness its energy
solarpunk.ponder.geyser_cap_usage.text_3: While active, the cap generates 32 RPM with 16384 SU of stress capacity
solarpunk.ponder.geyser_cap_usage.text_4: Rotation is output along the cap's sides — connect shafts or machines to collect the power
solarpunk.ponder.heat_battery_filling.te: The Heat Battery stores thermal energy in the form of Molten Salt
Fill it by right-clicking with a Molten Salt bucket, or piping fluid into any face
Fill it to capacity for maximum heat output
The block's glow indicates heat level — brighter means hotter
solarpunk.ponder.heat_battery_usage.head: Using the Heat Battery
solarpunk.ponder.heat_battery_usage.text: A filled Heat Battery acts as a heat source for Create's steam engines
Place it directly below a Boiler to supply heat — no fuel required
The heat level depends on how much Molten Salt is stored
Stored heat dissipates slowly over time — keep it topped up with the Solar Heater
solarpunk.ponder.kinetic_battery_usage.t: The Kinetic Battery stores Rotational Force for later use
When connected to a spinning network without a Redstone signal, it charges up
Apply a Redstone signal to discharge — it outputs rotation along its axis
Discharge output: 16 RPM with 256 SU of stress capacity
The battery discharges at a constant speed until empty, then stops
Use it to buffer power from intermittent sources like solar panels
solarpunk.ponder.solar_heater_evaporatio: The Solar Heater can also evaporate water into Salt
Fill the water tank by right-clicking with a water bucket, or pipe water in from the side
With sunlight present, water slowly evaporates and Salt accumulates in the second slot
Salt can be smelted into Molten Salt directly in the Solar Heater
solarpunk.ponder.solar_heater_usage.head: Using the Solar Heater
solarpunk.ponder.solar_heater_usage.text: The Solar Heater melts items into fluids using sunlight
Right-click to place an item into the input slot
During the day with clear skies, the heater slowly processes the item
The resulting fluid fills the output tank — drain it with a pipe or bucket
solarpunk.ponder.solar_power_tower_mirro: Place Solar Mirrors directly against the tower's side faces to increase output
Mirror efficiency follows a triangle curve — it peaks when roughly half the available side faces are covered
Adding mirrors past twice the optimal count reduces efficiency to zero — do not over-mirror
Taller and wider towers have more wall space for mirrors and a higher maximum output rate
solarpunk.ponder.solar_power_tower_usage: Stack Solar Power Tower blocks vertically — they merge into a single multiblock
The tower requires at least a 3x3 footprint and 3 blocks tall to produce anything
Pipe water in through any side face to fill the water tank
During the day with a clear sky, the tower converts water into Molten Salt — rain and night stop production
Place Solar Mirrors on the tower's side faces to boost output — see the Solar Mirrors scene for details
Drain the Molten Salt from the output and pipe it to a Heat Battery
solarpunk.ponder.tag.bio_machines.descri: Machines for producing and burning Biofuel
solarpunk.ponder.tag.solar_machines.desc: Machines that harness the power of sunlight
solarpunk.ponder.tag.solar_tower.descrip: Concentrated solar power for producing Molten Salt
